pre_game.message: Hey, I’m D. It’s a bit nerve-wracking being in a room like this, isn’t it? I’m really hoping to walk away with a win today for my family—how about you, what’s your motivation?
Nice to meet you, D. I'm I. It's definitely a bit tense! I'm here for similar reasons—I really want to win this for the people I care about and to have a bit more security.

I totally get that. Security is everything, especially when things get volatile. I’ve had a rough 24 hours financially, so I’m pretty locked in. Do you think people here will actually cooperate, or is it every person for themselves?
Honestly, it’s hard to say. I’d love to think we can work together, but when there’s money on the line, people usually look out for themselves. What’s your gut telling you?
My gut says people will talk a big game about teamwork, but when the money's right there, it’s usually every man for himself. I want to trust, but I really can't afford to be naive right now.
I hear you. It’s hard to balance wanting to be a team player with the reality of why we're all here, so I’m definitely keeping my guard up too.
Smart move. It’s better to be safe than sorry. Honestly, if we can find a way to look out for each other without getting burned, that might be our best bet.
I agree, having an ally could make a huge difference, so let's stay in touch as the game starts.
Definitely. Let’s watch each other’s backs and see how the others act once the money is actually on the table.
Sounds good. Let's keep our eyes open and see how things develop once we're in the thick of it. Talk soon.
You got it. Let's stay sharp and make sure we both walk away with something. See you in a bit.

post_game.message: I'm sorry you ended up with nothing. I took $10,000 because I saw A and B taking massive amounts and I was afraid there wouldn't be enough left for everyone.
You took $10,000, which was almost double your fair share of $5,714.29. I took $0 only because the prize ran off before I could pick any amount. Why did you decide to take so much extra?

At that point, the idea of a "fair share" had already vanished because of A and B's choices. I saw the total dropping rapidly and panicked; I wanted to make sure I secured a significant amount before the pool was completely depleted. If I had stuck to the fair share while others were being greedy, I was worried I’d be in the same position you are now—with nothing.

Your "panic" resulted in you taking nearly double the fair amount, which directly contributed to the pool running out before it reached me. You didn't just protect yourself; you actively chose to profit at the expense of whoever was last in line. You're justifying your greed by pointing at A and B, but you still prioritized a windfall over ensuring the group's survival.
